benefits.definition: "Garlic boosts immune system" refers to its ability to stimulate and strengthen the body's natural defenses by providing a rich source of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and immune-modulating properties, which collectively enhance the body's ability to fight off infections and diseases.

"Garlic reduces inflammation" refers to its ability to inhibit the production of certain inflammatory chemicals in the body, potentially providing relief from conditions associated with inflammation such as arthritis, thanks to the presence of active compounds like allicin.

"Garlic combats oxidation" refers to its ability to inhibit or reduce the process of oxidation, a chemical reaction that can produce harmful free radicals, by providing a rich source of antioxidant compounds such as allicin, which help protect the body's cells from damage caused by oxidative stress.

"Garlic lowers blood pressure" refers to its ability to reduce systolic and diastolic blood pressure levels in hypertensive individuals, making it a potential natural alternative for managing high blood pressure when consumed in dietary or supplemental forms.

"Garlic reduces cholesterol levels" refers to its ability to lower the amount of low-density lipoprotein (LDL), or "bad" cholesterol, in the blood, thereby decreasing the risk of developing heart disease.

"Garlic fights bacterial infections" refers to its ability to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and potentially help in treating certain bacterial infections, due to the presence of a compound called allicin, which has antimicrobial properties.

"Garlic supports cardiovascular health" refers to its ability to help maintain healthy blood pressure and cholesterol levels, reduce the risk of plaque formation in the arteries, and improve blood flow, all of which contribute to overall heart health.

"Garlic enhances athletic performance" refers to its ability to potentially improve physical capacity in sports or exercise, as suggested by some studies, which suggest that garlic supplementation could lead to enhanced endurance, reduced oxidative stress, and improved cardiovascular response to exercise, ultimately contributing to enhanced overall athletic performance.

"Garlic improves digestive system" refers to its ability to promote the overall health and functioning of the digestive tract, including enhancing the digestion and absorption of food, reducing inflammation, combating harmful bacteria, and providing antioxidant benefits, which collectively contribute to a healthy and well-functioning digestive system.

"Garlic fights against cancer cells" refers to its ability to inhibit the growth and proliferation of cancer cells, potentially suppressing the development and progression of various types of cancer, as supported by a range of scientific studies that suggest garlic's organosulfur compounds, such as allicin, have anti-carcinogenic properties.
